subject: Databases
title: Durability
description: A fast breakdown of Durability for builders.

hook.text: You got 'COMMIT successful.' The server crashes one second later. Is the data safe? Durability says yes.

buildup.text: Durability guarantees committed data survives crashes, power failures, and hardware issues.

discovery.text: The write-ahead log (WAL) is flushed to disk before COMMIT returns. The data is on stable storage.

twist.text: fsync forces data from OS buffers to disk. Without it, a power loss could still lose committed data.

climax.text: Some databases offer relaxed durability for speed: synchronous_commit = off in Postgres skips fsync at COMMIT.

punchline.text: Durability: committed means saved. Even after a crash.

quiz.question: What role does fsync play in database durability?
options: It forces data from operating system buffers to physical disk, ensuring it survives power loss
It synchronizes data between two database servers
It compresses data before writing to disk
